content.problemStatement:
A bracket sequence SSS is called dense if one of the following is true:


SSS is empty.
S=(X)S = (X)S=(X) where XXX is dense.
You are given a bracket sequence SSS. What is the **minimum** number of brackets you must remove to make it dense?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, the number of test cases. The description of the TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN, denoting the length of the bracket sequence.
The second line of each test case contains the bracket sequence SSS of length NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of brackets you must remove from SSS to make it dense.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3â‹…1053 \cdot 10^53â‹…105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Remove the 2nd2^{nd}2nd and 7th7^{th}7th bracket to obtain ((())), which is dense.
Test case 222: The given bracket sequence is already dense.
Test case 333: Remove the 2nd2^{nd}2nd, 4th4^{th}4th, 7th7^{th}7th, and 9th9^{th}9th bracket to obtain ((())), which is dense.
Test case 444: Remove the last 222 brackets to obtain ((())).
